mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales post-pandemic indicate sustained consumer demand, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or monthly RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns with hit rate > 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, high borrowing costs compress equity valuations and tighten financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: In instances where FEDFUNDS > 5%, the proportion of positive 5-day forward returns exceeds 50% or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales coinciding with high interest rates signals an overheating economy where consumer demand is met with tight monetary policy, historically preceding negative equity returns as recession risks mount.
failure_condition: The joint signal (high RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS > 5%) fails to produce negative average 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC of the interaction term is not statistically significant at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a rise in the unemployment rate from a low base signals impending economic slowdown, reducing earnings expectations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 3.8 and UNRATE rising over 3 months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion when UNRATE begins to rise
failure_condition: In regime where UNRATE is >3.8% and increasing, the mean 60-day forward return is not significantly negative, or the signal fails in out-of-sample periods with similar macro conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production signals expanding supply capacity and robust economic activity, supporting corporate profitability and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The predictive Rank IC of INDPRO growth on 20-day forward returns is less than 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the hit rate does not exceed 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth combined with unexpectedly weak retail sales suggests wage gains are not fueling consumption, signaling a disconnect that historically leads to negative equity returns as consumer spending falters.
failure_condition: The interaction of high PAYEMS growth (>0.2% MoM) and low RSAFS growth (<0.1% MoM) does not yield negative average 20-day forward returns with statistical significance (p<0.05) in a regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.3 million units signals a contraction in the residential investment cycle, weakening economic momentum and dragging down S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In periods where HOUST is below 1300, the average 60-day forward return is not significantly negative, or the strategy produces a Sharpe ratio below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment combined with stable or rising interest rates indicates the Federal Reserve is tightening into a strong labor market—a combination that historically precedes economic downturns and negative S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal (FEDFUNDS > 5% and UNRATE < 4%) does not produce negative mean 20-day forward returns with statistical significance, or fails to outperform a simple buy-and-hold in out-of-sample periods.
